title: Priorities
content:
I know this thing feels off and new, but I am writing this after I got into a little accident at my workplace while I was so eager to make money and be independent.

Sometimes we gotta give ourselves time for our health and our mind. My little accident happened when the janitor was mopping the floor while I was coming out of the lift, and the floor was so wet that I found myself on the ground before I even knew what had happened.

I am writing this while I have a simple headache, feeling sleepy, and yawning more than usual. Google and GPT said it could be a concussion. I will go to the clinic soon and all, but why I am here is just to say, **"Let's give ourselves time, and let's take care of our mind and our health!"**

What I realized is that the more I try to make money, the more God humbles me. I was in a bad situation where I kept asking myself, while everybody is making some money and all, why am I not getting any while I am putting in more effort than them? Are they just lucky? Or do I have to wait for my time? Or is it something else?

I was in that situation, trying to make sense of it, but at the end I understood that there is much more than that...
**Health.**

Yeah, health. Everything we have is precious and unique. I wasn't blind—I knew all this, but I couldn't appreciate it because I was only thinking about money and getting rich ASAP. I was more focused on the things I wanted to achieve than looking at myself, my family, and the people beside me.

The points may seem vague and unclear, and they may even seem absurd... But if there is one thing I want you to take from this, it's this:

Take care of yourself, guys.

**"No such thing as a life that's better than yours!"** — J. Cole

_(I love him, guys.)_
